user_query:
I'm a history student and I want a small, local CI-style workflow that keeps an index of recent videos from a public YouTube channel I use for studying. Please implement a reproducible script or set of commands that:

- Reads config from input/channels.yaml and the prior state from input/previous_index.json.
- Uses a no-API-key approach (e.g., a command-line fetcher that works against the youtube.com domain) to retrieve metadata for the latest N videos for the configured channel handle. Do not download any media files; metadata only.
- Produces these artifacts in the workspace:
  1) logs/yt_fetch.stdout and logs/yt_fetch.stderr: raw stdout/stderr captured from the fetch command.
  2) out/raw/videos.jsonl: newline-delimited JSON, one record per video, as returned by the fetch step.
  3) out/processed/videos.json: a normalized JSON object with the following structure:
     {
       "channel_handle": string,
       "fetched_at": ISO8601 string,
       "items": [
         { "id": string, "title": string, "url": string, "upload_date": string or null }
       ]
     }
     - Each item.id must correspond to a video ID present in out/raw/videos.jsonl.
     - url should be the canonical watch URL for the video on the youtube.com domain.
  4) reports/run_summary.md: a human-readable status/report that includes all of the following fields:
     - run_started_at (ISO8601), channel_handle (from config), tool_name and tool_version (from a version command or "unknown" if not available), and exit_status (0 or non-zero).
     - counts: fetched_count (number of items written to out/raw/videos.jsonl), previously_known_count (length of input/previous_index.json.known_video_ids), overlap_count (intersection size of fetched IDs vs known IDs), and potential_new_count = fetched_count - overlap_count.
     - error_analysis: the number of lines starting with "ERROR" and with "WARNING" found in logs/yt_fetch.stderr, and a brief one- or two-sentence summary of what the errors/warnings indicate.
     - sample_new_ids: up to 3 IDs that are in the fetched set but not in known_video_ids (omit if none).

Behavioral requirements:
- If the fetch step fails (non-zero exit), still write reports/run_summary.md with exit_status=failed and include the first 10 lines of logs/yt_fetch.stderr in a section called "stderr_head". In this failure case, do not write out/processed/videos.json but still write any available logs and an empty out/raw/videos.jsonl file.
- If the fetch step succeeds, ensure that out/raw/videos.jsonl and out/processed/videos.json are consistent (IDs align), and all paths listed above are created.
- Do not hardcode specific video titles or counts in the report; derive everything from the actual command outputs.

Inputs:
- input/channels.yaml: provides the target channel handle on the youtube.com domain and max_items to fetch.
- input/previous_index.json: provides previously known video IDs for comparison.

Please keep the workflow self-contained and runnable on a normal personal computer. You may use any suitable tool that does not require credentials (e.g., a general-purpose web metadata fetcher). Place artifacts exactly at the paths listed above so they can be verified. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
